text.title: Laravel Layered Architecture Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the clean-architecture layering for Laravel. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST contain only: FormRequest validation, single service call, JsonResource wrap, return `JsonResponse`. Eloquent queries and business logic in controllers are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Business logic in controllers cannot be unit-tested without booting HTTP; service isolation makes each concern independently testable.

rule.statement: Services MUST accept primitives, DTOs, or Eloquent models from the controller. `request()`, `$_GET`, `auth()`, `session()` accesses inside services are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Framework-coupled services cannot be exercised from queues, CLI commands, or unit tests without booting Laravel.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST call `FormRequest::validated()` and pass the result to the service. `$request->all()`, `$request->input()`, and `$request->merge(...)` for protected fields are forbidden.
rule.rationale: `validated()` returns only declared fields; `all()` lets clients inject arbitrary fields.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST return `JsonResource` / `ResourceCollection`. Services MUST return Eloquent models or DTOs — never `JsonResponse` or `JsonResource`.
rule.rationale: Leaking `JsonResponse` into the service layer couples it to HTTP; services must be callable from queue jobs and CLI commands.

rule.statement: Multi-write paths MUST use `DB::transaction(fn() => ...)`. Manual `DB::beginTransaction()` / `commit` / `rollBack` is forbidden. Wrapping the closure in a `try/catch` that swallows the exception is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Closure-based transactions auto-rollback on any exception; manual begin/commit requires remembering to rollback in every catch branch.

rule.statement: Add a Repository class ONLY when (a) multiple data sources exist, (b) you need to mock DB without a DB, or (c) explicit query encapsulation is required. Interfaces MUST be skipped until a second implementation exists.
rule.rationale: Eloquent is already an Active Record; wrapping it everywhere adds ceremony without value. LLMs over-prescribe repositories.
